title: Deliciously Tangy Argentinian Pork Skillet
description: A tangy and light Argentinian dish featuring pork shoulder steaks, this recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner. It is made with a mother-sauce-base and does not include onion or garlic.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large tava griddle over medium-high heat.
Add the pork slices and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked through.
Remove the pork from the griddle and set aside to rest.
In the same griddle, add the lemon juice, chicken broth, tomato paste, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper.
Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and let the sauce simmer for 5 minutes, or until slightly thickened.
Return the pork slices to the griddle and spoon some of the sauce over the top of each slice.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the pork and serve immediately.
